subject: Sociology
title: Education and Mobility
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Education and Mobility.

hook.text: A college degree used to guarantee a middle-class life. Now it guarantees debt and a fighting chance.

buildup.text: Education is the most studied path to upward mobility. Degrees still boost earnings, but the premium is uneven.

discovery.text: Children from wealthy families are far more likely to attend elite colleges, even with equal academic ability.

twist.text: First-generation students face hidden barriers: no guidance on applications, less financial aid, weaker networks.

climax.text: Education can be an equalizer or a reproducer of inequality, depending on who gets access to quality schools.

punchline.text: Education opens doors — but not the same doors for everyone.

quiz.question: How does education relate to social mobility?
options: It can equalize or reproduce inequality depending on access
It guarantees upward mobility for everyone equally
It has no effect on social position
